steps.title: Factor out the GCF
steps.content: The greatest common factor of $7x^{6}$ and $14x^{2}$ is $7x^{2}$. Pull it out: $$7x^{6}-14x^{2}=7x^{2}(x^{4}-2).$$

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• A: $7(x^{3}-2x^{2})^{2}$ expands to $7(x^{6}-4x^{5}+4x^{4})=7x^{6}-28x^{5}+28x^{4}$ — wrong.
  • B: $x^{2}(7x-2)^{4}$ uses a 4th power, producing terms like $2401x^{6}$ with extra middle terms — wrong.
  • C: $7x^{2}(x-2)^{4}$ also uses a 4th power and gives $7x^{6}-56x^{5}+\dots$ — wrong.
  • D: $7x^{2}(x^{4}-2)=7x^{6}-14x^{2}$ — matches.
D is correct.
